stocks.name: Butterfly Network
stocks.explanation: Butterfly Network shares fell sharply after a Form 144 insider selling filing appeared Monday, signaling planned executive share sales. The drop reverses gains from the recent Midjourney Scanner partnership announcement, which had boosted sentiment around the company’s ultrasound-on-chip technology. Thin float amplified the move on moderate volume.

stocks.name: Super Micro Computer
stocks.explanation: Super Micro surged after GF Securities upgraded the stock from Hold to Buy with a $48 price target. The upgrade followed SMCI unveiling a new AI data center platform powered by NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL4 technology at ISC 2026 in Hamburg, with commercial rollouts scheduled for H2 2026. The rally also represents a bounce from a nearly 28% drawdown caused by dilution concerns from a $7 billion equity raise earlier in June.

stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo bounced on improved large contract wins and stabilizing net retention signals, rebounding from steep year-to-date losses. The stock has been under pressure after the company cut full-year 2026 revenue guidance from $1.25B to $1.19B. Shares remain well off their 52-week high of $12.51, suggesting the move may be a short-covering rally rather than a fundamental inflection.

stocks.name: Roblox Corporation
stocks.explanation: Roblox continued its 2026 slide, now down over 36% year to date. The decline reflects ongoing fallout from the company slashing full-year bookings growth guidance to 8-12% from 22-26%, compounded by engagement headwinds from mandatory age verification changes. CEO David Baszucki has sold an estimated $38.6M in shares since February, adding to investor unease.

stocks.name: DigitalOcean Holdings
stocks.explanation: DigitalOcean pulled back amid broader tech weakness following its recent run-up from the S&P MidCap 400 addition and Hippocratic AI partnership. The stock had rallied sharply on its AI-focused cloud pivot, making it vulnerable to profit-taking. Low volume suggests the move may be amplified by thin liquidity rather than fundamental deterioration.

stocks.name: ON Semiconductor
stocks.explanation: ON Semiconductor extended its remarkable 2026 rally, now up over 137% year to date. AI data center segment revenue more than doubled year-over-year in Q1, and Q2 guidance topped consensus. The stock is riding the broader semiconductor supercycle, with any dips attracting institutional buying as ON positions itself as a critical enabler of AI power management infrastructure.

stocks.name: AST SpaceMobile
stocks.explanation: AST SpaceMobile pulled back alongside other space and satellite names as the post-SpaceX-IPO momentum faded. SpaceX shares fell 8% on Monday, dragging the broader space-adjacen
